---
title: "How Close Are Computers to Automating Mathematical Reasoning?"
description: "A high-level summary of automated theorem proving and where NLP and ML can help."
author: "synesis"
date: "2020-08-30"
categories: [mathematics, formal verification, NLP, links]
image: cover.jpg
---

![How close are computers to automating mathematical reasoning? Image from [Quanta Magazine](https://www.quantamagazine.org/how-close-are-computers-to-automating-mathematical-reasoning-20200827/).](cover.jpg){fig-align="center"}

A nice high-level summary of the current state of automated theorem proving: how it can be used to validate proofs done by humans, how translating math to computer languages remains a pain point, and how research in NLP/ML can help.


*Originally posted on [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/benjaminhan_how-close-are-computers-to-automating-mathematical-activity-6705915522673995776-zXnb).*
